name: Croatia
contextBlurb: Croatia allows retail use of stablecoins but they are not legal tender. Supervision is shifting to the EU’s MiCA regime: EMTs (fiat-referenced) and ARTs (asset-referenced) have applied EU-wide since 30 Jun 2024, with the remainder of MiCA in force since 30 Dec 2024; pre-existing CASPs may operate under the Art. 143(3) transitional regime until 1 Jul 2026. Locally, HANFA maintains the AML VASP register/notification (pre-MiCA) and will supervise MiCA authorisations; HNB covers e-money/payment matters. Crypto gains are taxed as capital income (generally 10% plus any local surcharge) on disposal. Overall, the framework is permissive but mid-transition, with full CASP licensing clarity expected by mid-2026.

legislation.title: Apr 2026 ESMA confirmed that the MiCA transitional period ends mid-2026; crypto firms must hold a full MiCA/CASP licence by that deadline. As of April 2026, 199 CASPs are licensed across the EU; no Asset-Referenced Token (ART) issuer has yet been approved.
MiCA fully applicable; pre-existing CASPs may operate under Art. 143(3) until 1 Jul 2026 (Croatia opted 18 months).
The Implementing Act enters into force (OG 85/2024), operationalising MiCA nationally.
ESMA publishes the list of competent authorities under MiCA showing HANFA/HNB as Croatia’s NCAs (by title).
Croatian Parliament passes the Act Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/1114 (MiCA).
MiCA stablecoin titles (ART/EMT) apply EU-wide, including in Croatia.

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Croatia allows retail use of stablecoins but they are not legal tender. Supervision is shifting to the EU’s MiCA regime: EMTs (fiat-referenced) and ARTs (asset-referenced) have applied EU-wide since 30 Jun 2024, with the remainder of MiCA in force since 30 Dec 2024; pre-existing CASPs may operate under the Art. 143(3) transitional regime until 1 Jul 2026. Locally, HANFA maintains the AML VASP register/notification (pre-MiCA) and will supervise MiCA authorisations; HNB covers e-money/payment matters. Crypto gains are taxed as capital income (generally 10% plus any local surcharge) on disposal. Overall, the framework is permissive but mid-transition, with full CASP licensing clarity expected by mid-2026.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Under the EU MiCA regime, Croatia classifies stablecoins as e-money tokens (EMTs) and asset-referenced tokens (ARTs).
